items.title: Color and contrast: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? A contrast-safe text hex value remains compliant on every translucent or image background.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Measure the rendered foreground-background pair in each state; a color value alone has no contrast ratio.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Measure the rendered foreground-background pair in each state; a color value alone has no contrast ratio.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

items.title: Color and contrast: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Choose foreground, background, focus and state colors that satisfy contrast and do not encode meaning alone. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating foreground/background and control contrast; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: /* TODO: implement the declared bounded CSS facet. */

answer.referenceSolution: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: rgb(25 25 30); background: rgb(255 255 255); } .copy { max-inline-size: 65ch; text-align: start; } .action { color: white; background: rgb(0 70 150); border: 2px solid currentColor; box-shadow: 0 .125rem .25rem rgb(0 0 0 / .25); }
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded css artifact for foreground/background and control contrast. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
